title: teucrium heterophyllum subsp. hierrense
intro: Teucrium heterophyllum subsp. hierrense, also known as Hierren's teucrium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Teucrium heterophyllum subsp. hierrense can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any health condition.
